tool_name: Cathodic Protection Design — Sacrificial Anode Current Demand, Mass & Service Life

inputs.name: Coating Breakdown Factor at End of Life (f_c)
inputs.note: DNV-RP-B401 Table 10-1: well-coated new: 3–5%; aged coating: 10–25%; bare/uncoated: 100%

inputs.name: Design Current Density (i_c) — Mean Value
inputs.note: DNV-RP-B401 Table 10-3: North Sea seabed ≈ 20–25 mA/m²; tropical shallow water ≈ 50–80 mA/m²; soil ≈ 10–30 mA/m²
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Cathodic Protection Design Life (T_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sacrificial Anode Material

options.label: Zinc (seawater) — ε=780 Ah/kg, E=−1.05 V vs Ag/AgCl

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Anode Utilisation Factor (u)
inputs.note: DNV-RP-B401 §6.4: bracelet anodes u=0.80; flush-mounted u=0.90; stand-off u=0.85

inputs.name: Target Protection Potential (E_prot)

options.label: −900 mV — Elevated temperature or H₂S service

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_eff_m2 = structure_area_m2 * coating_breakdown_pct / 100  // [m²] — Exposed (Uncoated) Effective Area (A_eff) — Ref: A_eff = A_s × f_c — DNV-RP-B401 §6.2; coating breakdown factor applied to bare area
Ic_mean_A = A_eff_m2 * current_density_mA_m2 / 1000  // [A] — Mean Current Demand (I_c,mean) — Ref: I_c = i_c × A_eff — DNV-RP-B401 §6.3.1 Eq.(6.1)
Q_T_Ah = Ic_mean_A * design_life_yr * 8760  // [A·h] — Total Charge Required Over Design Life (Q_T) — Ref: Q_T = I_c,mean × T_d × 8760 — total coulombs required over design life

const ECA = {Zn:780, AlIn:2000, Mg:1230}[anode_material]; Ma_kg = Q_T_Ah / (ECA * anode_utilisation_pct / 100)  // [kg] — Total Anode Net Mass Required (M_a) — Ref: M_a = Q_T / (ε × u) — DNV-RP-B401 §6.4 Eq.(6.3); ε = electrochemical capacity in Ah/kg

anode_std_mass_kg = (anode_material === 'Mg') ? 5 : 15; N_a = Math.ceil(Ma_kg / anode_std_mass_kg)  // [count] — Number of Standard Anodes Required (N_a) — Ref: N_a = M_a / m_std; standard bracelet anode: Al-In 15 kg; Mg 5 kg net mass — DNV Table 10-4

const E_anode = {Zn:-1050, AlIn:-1050, Mg:-1550}[anode_material]; dE_mv = E_anode - protection_potential_mv; resistance_est_ohm = 0.315 * Math.sqrt(A_eff_m2 / N_a) / 10; I_output_A = dE_mv / 1000 / resistance_est_ohm  // [mV, A] — Driving Voltage & Current Output Check — Ref: Driving voltage ΔE = E_anode − E_prot; current = ΔE/R_a; R_a from Dwight formula — DNV-RP-B401 §7

rules.message: Bare steel with low current density: verify environment — bare steel in stagnant seawater may require 50–100 mA/m² per DNV Table 10-3
Zinc anodes at high current density: driving voltage insufficient for tropical warm water — switch to Al-In alloy with higher electrochemical efficiency
Total anode mass > 100 tonnes: very large CP system — evaluate impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP) for structures > 20,000 m²; lower OPEX despite higher CAPEX

smart_warnings.message: Zinc anodes for 20+ year design life: mass penalty is severe — Al-In provides 2.6× more charge per kg at same potential; mandatory for deepwater and long-life structures per DNV §5.2
Driving voltage < 150 mV: anode current output will be marginal — verify polarisation resistance of structure; may need to increase anode count by 40% for adequate protection
Over-protection risk with Mg anodes (E < −900 mV): hydrogen embrittlement possible on high-strength steels (Fy > 550 MPa) — limit to −950 mV per NACE SP0169 §6.2.2
Large exposed area with few anodes: current distribution will be uneven — increase anode count and space evenly; maximum spacing = 3 m for bare surfaces per DNV §10.3
Low coating breakdown (<3%): CP demand is minimal — verify coating integrity with holiday detection per NACE SP0188; CP as backup protection only

fmea.effect: Bare steel with low current density: verify environment — bare steel in stagnant seawater may require 50–100 mA/m² per DNV Table 10-3
Zinc anodes at high current density: driving voltage insufficient for tropical warm water — switch to Al-In alloy with higher electrochemical efficiency
Total anode mass > 100 tonnes: very large CP system — evaluate impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP) for structures > 20,000 m²; lower OPEX despite higher CAPEX

fmea.failureMode: Zinc anodes for 20+ year design life: mass penalty is severe — Al-In provides 2.6× more charge per kg at same potential; mandatory for deepwater and long-life structures per DNV §5.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_life_yr > 20 && anode_material === 'Zn'

fmea.failureMode: Driving voltage < 150 mV: anode current output will be marginal — verify polarisation resistance of structure; may need to increase anode count by 40% for adequate protection
Over-protection risk with Mg anodes (E < −900 mV): hydrogen embrittlement possible on high-strength steels (Fy > 550 MPa) — limit to −950 mV per NACE SP0169 §6.2.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: protection_potential_mv < -900 && anode_material === 'Mg'

fmea.failureMode: Large exposed area with few anodes: current distribution will be uneven — increase anode count and space evenly; maximum spacing = 3 m for bare surfaces per DNV §10.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_eff_m2 > 5000 && N_a < 20

fmea.failureMode: Low coating breakdown (<3%): CP demand is minimal — verify coating integrity with holiday detection per NACE SP0188; CP as backup protection only
